message:
feat(cli): expand ffs-cli with interactive commands and filesystem inspection tooling

Significantly expand ffs-cli/src/main.rs (+401 lines) with new
subcommands for filesystem inspection, block-level diagnostics,
and interactive mount management. Add new dependencies to
ffs-cli/Cargo.toml and update Cargo.lock accordingly.

Sync beads issue tracker metadata.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -19,7 +19,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-15c.2","title":"Implement on-image repair symbol storage (per block group)","description":"# Implement on-image repair symbol storage (per block group)

## GOAL
Persist RaptorQ repair symbols (and any required validation metadata) in a **durable, checksummed on-image format** so recovery can run after restart.

## CURRENT REALITY (CODE)
- `crates/ffs-repair/src/symbol.rs` already defines:
  - `RepairBlockHeader` (per repair block, CRC32C checked)
  - `RepairGroupDescExt` (OTI + repair region metadata, CRC32C checked)
  - constants and deterministic seed helpers
- There is **no** implemented I/O layer that:
  - reserves blocks for repair data
  - writes/reads symbol blocks
  - enforces atomic generation updates

## STORAGE STRATEGY (V1)
Use an **in-image reserved range at the end of each block group**, consistent with the layout described in `crates/ffs-repair/src/symbol.rs`.

Key requirement for ext4 images:
- Reserved repair blocks must be marked **allocated** in the ext4 block bitmap (and reflected in free-block counters), so the kernel allocator will never hand them out.

## SCOPE
1. Finalize the exact per-group layout:
   - how many blocks are reserved for:
     - validation digests (optional, but strongly recommended)
     - repair symbols
     - group metadata (`RepairGroupDescExt`) if stored in-band
   - which exact block(s) store `RepairGroupDescExt` and how it is located deterministically.
2. Implement the storage I/O layer (likely new module under `crates/ffs-repair/src/`):
   - `read_group_desc_ext(group) -> RepairGroupDescExt`
   - `write_group_desc_ext(group, new_ext) -> ()` (atomic update rules)
   - `read_repair_symbols(group) -> Vec<(esi, bytes)>`
   - `write_repair_symbols(group, symbols, generation) -> ()`
3. Implement crash-safety for updates:
   - write all new symbol blocks first
   - only then advance the durable generation counter (so torn writes are detectable)
   - on open: choose the latest fully-valid generation
4. Integrate with ext4 metadata accounting (phase-gated but must be planned here):
   - ensure reserved blocks are excluded from allocation (`bd-huh.2`)
   - ensure group/superblock counters remain consistent

## TESTS (REQUIRED)
1. Unit tests for serialization/parsing (already partially present):
   - roundtrip `RepairBlockHeader` and `RepairGroupDescExt`
   - checksum mismatch detection
2. Integration tests using an in-memory `BlockDevice`:
   - write a full group’s symbol set
   - read it back and verify byte-equality
   - simulate torn write (only half the blocks written) and assert the reader rejects the incomplete generation
3. Logging requirements:
   - tests must log group number, generation, and which block offsets were written/read on failure

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. Repair symbol blocks can be written and read back deterministically.
2. Torn/partial updates are detected and do not silently “half-apply”.
3. The storage layer is usable by the recovery pipeline (`bd-15c.3`).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:01:32.860960386Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T02:09:14.762904446Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T02:09:14.762789360Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","io","ondisk","repair"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-15c.2","depends_on_id":"bd-15c","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:01:32.860960386Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-15c.2","depends_on_id":"bd-15c.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:01:57.631365276Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-15c.3","title":"Implement corruption recovery pipeline","description":"# Implement corruption recovery pipeline

## GOAL
Wire together:
- corruption detection signals
- symbol retrieval (bd-15c.2)
- RaptorQ decode (`ffs-repair` codec)
- block repair writeback
- post-repair verification

…into an end-to-end, auditable recovery flow.

## CURRENT REALITY (CODE)
- `crates/ffs-repair/src/codec.rs` provides deterministic `encode_group` / `decode_group`.
- `crates/ffs-repair/src/symbol.rs` defines a checksummed symbol format.
- `crates/ffs-repair/src/scrub.rs` provides a generic scrub engine that can surface corruption findings.

What’s missing is the actual **orchestration** and the integration point where reads/scrubs trigger recovery.

## SCOPE
1. Implement a recovery orchestrator (likely new module under `crates/ffs-repair/src/`):
   - determine block group boundaries
   - identify corrupt block indices within the group
   - load repair symbols for the group
   - run `decode_group()` and obtain recovered blocks
   - write recovered bytes back to the device
   - re-validate repaired blocks
2. Define a minimal corruption signal model for V1:
   - Phase A (required for tests): explicit corrupt index list (test-driven)
   - Phase B (recommended): integrate with validation metadata (if bd-15c.2 stores digests) or format-specific validators
3. Evidence ledger (non-negotiable per AGENTS.md):
   - every recovery attempt must emit a structured record with:
     - group, generation
     - corrupt_count
     - symbols_available / symbols_used
     - decoder stats (`DecodeStats`)
     - outcome (recovered / partial / failed + reason)

## INTEGRATION POINTS (CHOOSE ONE; PREFER MINIMAL)
- Option A (preferred): a `RecoveringBlockDevice` wrapper in `ffs-block` that:
  - delegates to an inner `BlockDevice`
  - on read, runs a validator and triggers recovery on mismatch
- Option B: a CLI-driven repair path (`ffs scrub --repair` or similar) that:
  - scrubs blocks
  - repairs findings
  - re-scrubs to prove clean

The bead must pick one and make it testable.

## TESTS (REQUIRED)
1. End-to-end in-memory test:
   - create deterministic group blocks
   - encode + store repair symbols
   - corrupt N blocks (overwrite bytes)
   - run recovery
   - assert the original bytes are restored for all corrupted blocks
2. Failure-mode test:
   - corrupt more blocks than redundancy supports
   - assert recovery fails gracefully (no partial silent writes)
3. Evidence ledger test:
   - assert the ledger record contains all required fields and is parseable JSON

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. Recovery succeeds when redundancy is sufficient and restores exact bytes.
2. Recovery fails loudly and safely when redundancy is insufficient.
3. Evidence ledger is produced for every attempt and is actionable.
4. No panics on malformed symbol data; errors are explicit.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:01:52.376968324Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T02:16:36.980290155Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T02:16:36.980203793Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","io","repair","semantics"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-15c.3","depends_on_id":"bd-15c","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:01:52.376968324Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-15c.3","depends_on_id":"bd-15c.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:01:57.721519356Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-15c.3","depends_on_id":"bd-15c.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:01:57.813402514Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-15c.4","title":"Add E2E corruption-injection + recovery verification scripts","description":"# Add E2E corruption-injection + recovery verification scripts

## GOAL
Add an end-to-end script that:
1. Produces (or copies) a working ext4 fixture image
2. Generates/stores repair symbols
3. Injects controlled corruption into a bounded set of blocks
4. Runs a read/scrub path that triggers recovery
5. Verifies the recovered bytes match the pre-corruption baseline
6. Captures an evidence ledger + detailed logs

## CONTEXT
Unit tests can validate encode/decode and parsing, but recovery is an end-to-end behavior across:
- block I/O
- checksum validation
- symbol store
- RaptorQ decode
- repair writeback

This bead makes recovery auditable and reproducible.

## DELIVERABLES
1. `scripts/e2e/ffs_repair_recovery_smoke.sh`
2. Uses `scripts/e2e/lib.sh` logging conventions.
3. Produces artifacts under `artifacts/e2e/<timestamp>/repair/`:
   - `before_checksums.txt`
   - `after_checksums.txt`
   - `corruption_plan.json`
   - `recovery_evidence.jsonl`

## CORRUPTION INJECTION RULES
- Inject corruption deterministically using a fixed seed.
- Corrupt a small percentage (for example 1-3%) of blocks in a single group.
- Record exactly which blocks were modified and how.
- Corruption method must be reversible/controlled (bit flips, zeroing, truncation is not acceptable unless explicitly tested).

## VERIFICATION
- Before corruption, compute `sha256sum` of a bounded set of files read through FUSE (or read via CLI read-path if available).
- After recovery, recompute and verify identical.
- Additionally verify block-level checksums where available.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. Script is deterministic and idempotent.
2. Logs include enough detail to reproduce a failure.
3. Evidence ledger exists and is easy to parse (JSONL).
4. Script skips gracefully (exit 0) if repair pipeline is not available yet, but prints a clear reason.

## NOTES
- This bead does not decide the recovery policy; it validates the implementation of bd-15c.3.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T20:57:01.928728898Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:31.212221106Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["harness","repair"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-15c.4","depends_on_id":"bd-15c","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T20:57:01.928728898Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-15c.4","depends_on_id":"bd-15c.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T20:57:01.928728898Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-15c.4","depends_on_id":"bd-15c.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:51:49.782332022Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-15c.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2jk.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T20:57:01.928728898Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-15c.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2jk.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:53:49.210324359Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":197,"issue_id":"bd-15c.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

Cluster: quality_assurance
Mapped graveyard sections: 0.1 Optimization Loop + 0.7 Artifact Contract + 6.12 Proptest
EV score: 50 (Impact*Confidence*Reuse / (Effort*Friction))
Priority tier: A
Budgeted mode: bench_runs>=10, sample_floor=30 for noisy paths
Fallback trigger: golden-only gate, disable adaptive controller deployment

Required proof artifacts for execution on this bead:
- Baseline + hotspot evidence before changes.
- Isomorphism proof block per lever.
- Evidence-ledger event for decision path + fallback invocation.
- Repro pack: env.json, manifest.json, repro.lock (and LEGAL.md if IP risk appears).","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:31Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-15c.5","title":"Implement background scrub daemon with Region-based scheduling","description":"# Implement background scrub daemon with Region-based scheduling

## GOAL
Launch an asupersync Region that performs continuous background scrub of all block groups, detecting corruption and triggering automatic RaptorQ recovery.

## BACKGROUND
From README: "Corruption detected by checksums triggers automatic recovery. No separate fsck pass. No downtime."

The existing pieces:
- `ffs-repair::scrub::scrub_group()` - scans one group ✅
- `ffs-repair::recovery::GroupRecoveryOrchestrator` - recovers one group ✅
- `ffs-repair::storage::RepairGroupStorage` - reads/writes symbols ✅

What's missing: the daemon loop that:
1. Iterates groups in round-robin
2. Calls scrub_group() on each
3. On corruption: triggers GroupRecoveryOrchestrator
4. Respects backpressure (yield under I/O pressure)
5. Produces structured evidence log

## DESIGN
```rust
pub struct ScrubDaemon {
    region: asupersync::Region,
    interval: Duration,
    state: Arc<ScrubState>, // tracks last-scanned group, stats
    metrics: ScrubMetrics,
}

impl ScrubDaemon {
    pub fn spawn(cx: &Cx, engine: Arc<FrankenFsEngine>) -> Self {
        // Launch Region that:
        // 1. Iterates groups 0..N
        // 2. scrub_group() each
        // 3. On corruption: attempt_recovery()
        // 4. Sleep(interval) between rounds
        // 5. Yield if cx.is_cancelled() or backpressure
    }
}
```

## DELIVERABLES
1. `ScrubDaemon` struct in ffs-repair or ffs-core
2. Region-based async scheduling via asupersync
3. Automatic recovery trigger on corruption detection
4. Structured JSON evidence log for all scrub/recovery events
5. Configurable scan interval and backpressure sensitivity

## TESTS (REQUIRED)
1. Unit: Daemon starts and scans at least one group
2. Unit: Daemon detects injected corruption and triggers recovery
3. Unit: Daemon respects cancellation (stops promptly)
4. Unit: Daemon yields under backpressure (doesn't block reads)
5. Integration: Evidence log captures all events
6. Integration: Full filesystem scrub completes without error
7. Stress: Scrub under concurrent I/O load

## LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
- Daemon start (info): total_groups, interval_secs, config
- Round start (debug): round_number, starting_group
- Group scan start (trace): group_id
- Group scan complete (trace): group_id, duration_ms, blocks_scanned, errors_found
- Corruption detected (warn): group_id, block_numbers, checksum_type, expected_vs_actual
- Recovery started (info): group_id, corrupt_block_count, available_symbols
- Recovery success (info): group_id, blocks_recovered, duration_ms
- Recovery failure (error): group_id, reason, remaining_corrupt
- Backpressure yield (debug): current_group, pressure_source, yield_duration_ms
- Round complete (info): round_number, duration_secs, groups_scanned, total_corrupt, total_recovered
- Daemon stop (info): reason, final_stats
- Evidence entry (JSON): structured event for each scrub/recovery action

## METRICS (for TUI dashboard)
- blocks_scanned_total: Counter
- blocks_corrupt_found: Counter
- blocks_recovered: Counter
- blocks_unrecoverable: Counter
- scrub_rounds_completed: Counter
- current_group: Gauge
- scrub_rate_blocks_per_sec: Gauge

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. Background scrub runs continuously during mount
2. Corruption is detected and recovered automatically
3. No noticeable performance impact on foreground I/O (<5% throughput reduction)
4. Clean shutdown on unmount (in-flight group completes)
5. All events logged in structured format","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:51:36.870182966Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:17.644621212Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["daemon","repair","scrub"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-15c.5","depends_on_id":"bd-15c","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:51:36.870182966Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-15c.5","depends_on_id":"bd-15c.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:51:49.664141148Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-15c.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1ymg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:53:30.131634757Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":37,"issue_id":"bd-15c.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"ALIEN-ARTIFACT QUALITY: The scrub daemon embodies one of FrankenFS's core innovations—continuous self-healing. Design this with Bayesian evidence accumulation: track per-group corruption rates over time, adjust scan frequency based on observed failure rates (groups with more corruption get scanned more often). This is the principled alternative to fixed-interval scanning.","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:57:33Z"},{"id":114,"issue_id":"bd-15c.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

Cluster: general
Mapped graveyard sections: 0.8 Runtime Decision Core + 0.19 Evidence Ledger
EV score: 9 (Impact*Confidence*Reuse / (Effort*Friction))
Priority tier: B
Budgeted mode: controller_tick_ms=1000, policy_timeout_ms=200
Fallback trigger: static config profile

Required proof artifacts for execution on this bead:
- Baseline + hotspot evidence before changes.
- Isomorphism proof block per lever.
- Evidence-ledger event for decision path + fallback invocation.
- Repro pack: env.json, manifest.json, repro.lock (and LEGAL.md if IP risk appears).","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:17Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-15c.6","title":"Wire automatic corruption recovery into scrub detection pipeline","description":"# Wire automatic corruption recovery into scrub detection pipeline

## GOAL
When scrub_group() detects a checksum mismatch, automatically trigger GroupRecoveryOrchestrator to recover the corrupted block using RaptorQ repair symbols.

## BACKGROUND
Currently scrub detects corruption (returns mismatch list) but the caller has to manually invoke recovery. This bead connects the two so corruption → recovery is automatic.

## DELIVERABLES
1. `ScrubWithRecovery` struct that wraps scrub + recovery
2. On mismatch: load repair symbols, attempt decode, write back corrected block
3. On successful recovery: refresh repair symbols (re-encode group)
4. On failed recovery: log evidence, mark block as unrecoverable
5. Return RecoveryReport with per-block outcomes

## DESIGN
```rust
pub struct ScrubWithRecovery {
    scrubber: GroupScrubber,
    recovery: GroupRecoveryOrchestrator,
    evidence_log: EvidenceLog,
}

impl ScrubWithRecovery {
    pub async fn scrub_and_recover(
        &mut self, 
        group: GroupNumber, 
        cx: &Cx
    ) -> Result<RecoveryReport> {
        // 1. Scrub group
        let findings = self.scrubber.scrub_group(group, cx).await?;
        
        // 2. If corruption found, attempt recovery
        if !findings.corrupt_blocks.is_empty() {
            let recovery_result = self.recovery
                .recover_group(group, &findings.corrupt_blocks, cx)
                .await;
            
            // 3. Log evidence regardless of outcome
            self.evidence_log.record(RecoveryEvidence {
                timestamp: Utc::now(),
                group,
                corrupt_blocks: findings.corrupt_blocks.clone(),
                recovery_result: recovery_result.clone(),
            });
            
            // 4. If recovery succeeded, refresh symbols
            if recovery_result.is_ok() {
                self.refresh_repair_symbols(group, cx).await?;
            }
        }
        
        Ok(RecoveryReport { ... })
    }
}
```

## TESTS (REQUIRED)
1. Unit: Single corrupted block → automatic recovery → verified correct
2. Unit: Multiple corrupted blocks in same group → all recovered
3. Unit: Too many corrupted blocks (beyond RaptorQ capacity) → graceful failure report
4. Unit: Recovery of stale block (written after symbol gen) → expected failure with clear error
5. Integration: Full group corruption and recovery cycle
6. Integration: Evidence log contains all required fields
7. Stress: 100 groups with random corruption patterns

## LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
### Scrub phase
- scrub_start (debug): group
- mismatch_found (warn): group, block, expected_csum, actual_csum, csum_type

### Recovery phase
- recovery_start (info): group, corrupt_count, available_symbols
- symbol_load (trace): group, symbol_count, load_time_ms
- decode_attempt (debug): group, input_symbols, corrupt_positions
- decode_success (info): group, blocks_recovered, decode_time_ms
- decode_failure (error): group, reason, missing_symbols_needed
- block_writeback (trace): block, csum_after
- post_verify (debug): group, verify_result

### Symbol refresh
- refresh_start (debug): group, trigger (recovery/write)
- refresh_complete (info): group, symbols_generated, duration_ms

### Evidence logging (JSON structured)
```json
{
  "event": "recovery_attempt",
  "timestamp": "2026-02-13T04:00:00Z",
  "group": 42,
  "corrupt_blocks": [1024, 1025],
  "repair_symbols_available": 164,
  "decode_success": true,
  "blocks_recovered": 2,
  "blocks_unrecoverable": 0,
  "post_verify_ok": true,
  "total_duration_ms": 250
}
```

### Error logging
- io_error (error): operation, block, errno
- symbol_storage_error (error): group, slot, error
- checksum_mismatch_after_recovery (error): block, expected, actual (critical bug indicator)

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. Zero manual intervention for recoverable corruption
2. Evidence ledger captures every decision
3. Unrecoverable corruption clearly reported (not silent)
4. Post-recovery verification catches any recovery bugs
5. Symbol refresh prevents cascade failures","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"HazyCave","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:51:36.996553085Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T17:51:19.910038282Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T17:51:19.909947301Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-15c.6","depends_on_id":"bd-15c","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:51:36.996553085Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":46,"issue_id":"bd-15c.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"EVIDENCE LEDGER: Every recovery attempt must produce a JSON evidence record: {timestamp, group, corrupted_blocks, repair_symbols_available, decode_success, blocks_recovered, blocks_unrecoverable, post_verify_checksum_ok}.  {"id":"bd-15c.7","title":"Add comprehensive unit tests for self-healing repair pipeline","description":"# Add comprehensive unit tests for self-healing repair pipeline

## GOAL
Exhaustive unit test coverage for the RaptorQ encode/decode, scrub, storage, and recovery pipeline.

## DELIVERABLES
1. Codec tests: encode group, corrupt N blocks, decode succeeds for N ≤ symbol count
2. Codec boundary tests: exactly at repair capacity, 1 over capacity → failure
3. Storage tests: dual-slot generation commit, torn-write detection
4. Scrub tests: checksum mismatch detection for CRC32C and BLAKE3
5. Recovery tests: full pipeline (detect → load symbols → decode → writeback → verify)
6. Evidence tests: all recovery decisions captured in JSON ledger

## TESTS (≥20 individual test cases)
- Encode/decode roundtrip for various corruption patterns
- Boundary conditions (0 corruption, max corruption, over max)
- Storage generation rollover
- Scrub on clean data (no false positives)
- Recovery evidence ledger completeness

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. ≥20 unit tests for repair pipeline
2. All RaptorQ boundary conditions tested
3.  {"id":"bd-15c.8","title":"Implement evidence persistence for repair actions (JSONL)","description":"# Implement evidence persistence for repair actions (JSONL)

## GOAL
Every repair action, corruption detection, policy decision, and scrub cycle must produce a durable, auditable evidence record persisted as append-only JSONL (JSON Lines).

## BACKGROUND
The alien artifact methodology requires an auditable evidence ledger for every decision the system makes. This is non-negotiable for FrankenFS's self-healing story. Without durable evidence, repairs are invisible black boxes — unacceptable for a filesystem.

## DESIGN

### Record Schema
Each JSONL line is a self-contained JSON object with:
- timestamp_ns: u64 — monotonic nanosecond timestamp
- event_type: enum { CorruptionDetected, RepairAttempted, RepairSucceeded, RepairFailed, ScrubCycleComplete, PolicyDecision, SymbolRefresh }
- block_group: u32 — affected block group number
- block_range: (u64, u64) — start/end block numbers
- corruption_details: { blocks_affected: u32, checksum_expected: [u8;32], checksum_found: [u8;32] }
- repair_details: { symbols_used: u32, symbols_available: u32, decode_time_us: u64, verify_pass: bool }
- policy_context: { corruption_posterior: f64, overhead_ratio: f64, risk_bound: f64 }
- outcome: enum { Success, InsufficientRedundancy, IoError, Timeout }

### Persistence Strategy
- Append-only JSONL file per filesystem instance
- File location: reserved metadata area or configurable path
- fsync after each critical record (repair success/failure)
- Batch fsync for scrub cycle records (performance)
- Rotation: new file per mount session, old files archived

### Integration Points
- ffs-repair/src/scrub.rs — scrub daemon emits ScrubCycleComplete records
- ffs-repair/src/codec.rs — repair pipeline emits RepairAttempted/Succeeded/Failed
- Bayesian autopilot — emits PolicyDecision records when adjusting overhead
- Mount/unmount — session boundary markers

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] Every corruption detection produces a JSONL evidence record
- [ ] Every repair attempt (success or failure) produces a JSONL evidence record
- [ ] Every scrub cycle produces a summary JSONL record
- [ ] Records survive crash (fsync on critical events)
- [ ] ffs-cli evidence show can pretty-print the ledger
- [ ] Records are machine-parseable (valid JSON per line)
- [ ] Unit tests: serialize/deserialize round-trip for all event types","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"HazyCave","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:19:31.155123171Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T17:50:33.672096188Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T17:50:33.671995168Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["evidence","jsonl","repair"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-15c.8","depends_on_id":"bd-15c","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:19:31.155123171Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Provide unit test coverage for the ext4 Read Path Completion epic (bd-1xe). Currently has NO unit test bead and NO E2E test bead. The epic has 3 closed children and 1 in-progress, but none are test beads.

## TEST PLAN

### Journal Replay Tests
1. Replay empty journal: no-op
2. Replay single committed transaction: blocks written back
3. Replay aborted transaction: blocks discarded
4. Replay with torn write: partial transaction discarded (CRC check)
5. Replay ordering: transactions applied in sequence order

### Allocator Bitmap Tests
6. Read block bitmap: free/used status correct
7. Read inode bitmap: free/used status correct
8. Free block count matches bitmap popcount
9. Reserved blocks excluded from free count

### Orphan List Tests
10. Empty orphan list: no orphans
11. Orphan inode detected: inode in orphan list but allocated
12. Orphan chain traversal: follows linked list correctly

## TESTS (REQUIRED)
All 12 tests above. Use golden fixture images from bd-2jk.1.

## LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
- Test failures log block/inode numbers and expected vs actual state

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. All 12 tests pass
2. Tests use existing ext4 golden fixtures
3. -{"id":"bd-1ymg","title":"Implement structured logging with JSON output","description":"# Implement structured logging with JSON output

## GOAL
Implement production-grade structured logging for operational visibility and debugging.

## BACKGROUND
From AGENTS.md: "Deterministic audit logs for explainability."
From spec: "Evidence ledger (what evidence drove what decision)."

## TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

### 1. Structured Log Events
```rust
#[derive(Serialize)]
pub struct LogEvent {
    pub timestamp: DateTime<Utc>,
    pub level: Level,
    pub target: String,
    pub message: String,
    pub fields: BTreeMap<String, Value>,
    pub span: Option<SpanInfo>,
}
```

### 2. Domain-Specific Fields
```rust
// MVCC events
log_event!(level: INFO, 
    message: "transaction_commit",
    txn_id: txn.id,
    commit_seq: cs,
    write_set_size: txn.write_set.len(),
    duration_us: duration.as_micros(),
);

// Cache events
log_event!(level: DEBUG,
    message: "cache_eviction",
    block: block_num,
    reason: reason,
    t1_len: cache.t1_len(),
    t2_len: cache.t2_len(),
);
```

### 3. Span Context
```rust
let span = span!(Level::INFO, "mount", 
    image = %path, 
    mode = %if ro { "ro" } else { "rw" }
);
let _guard = span.enter();
// All logs within have span context
```

### 4. Output Formats
- Human-readable for development
- JSON for production/aggregation
- Configurable via env var or config

### 5. Log Rotation
- Size-based rotation
- Time-based rotation
- Compression of old logs

## TESTS
1. Unit: JSON output valid
2. Unit: Fields correctly serialized
3. Integration: Span context propagated
4. Integration: Rotation works

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. All significant events logged
2. JSON parseable by log aggregators
3. Span context maintained
4.  {"id":"bd-20p","title":"Implement multi-threaded FUSE dispatch with Send+Sync state","description":"# Implement multi-threaded FUSE dispatch with Send+Sync state

## GOAL
Enable concurrent FUSE request handling using fuser multi-threaded mode, as mandated by spec §1.7 Mechanical Sympathy.

## BACKGROUND
The spec states: "MUST use multi-threaded dispatch. All `Filesystem` state MUST be `Send + Sync`. `parking_lot` locks for concurrency."

Single-threaded FUSE dispatch is a severe bottleneck:
- 5-10μs per kernel/userspace round-trip
- Sequential ops block on I/O
- CPU underutilization on multi-core

## TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

### 1. FuseBackend State Architecture
```rust
pub struct FuseBackend {
    // Arc-wrapped for multi-threaded access
    inner: Arc<FuseInner>,
}

struct FuseInner {
    fs: RwLock<OpenFs>,           // parking_lot RwLock
    cache: Arc<ArcCache>,          // Already Send+Sync
    mvcc: Arc<MvccStore>,          // Already Send+Sync
    config: MountConfig,           // Immutable after mount
    metrics: AtomicMetrics,        // Lock-free counters
}
```

### 2. Thread Pool Configuration
- Default: min(num_cpus, 8) threads
- Configurable via MountConfig
- Each thread has its own I/O budget tracking

### 3. Lock Granularity
- ArcCache: Internal fine-grained locking (already done)
- MvccStore: Per-block version chain locks
- OpenFs: Per-operation RwLock (readers concurrent, writers exclusive)
- Metrics: Atomic counters (no locks)

### 4. Avoid False Sharing
- Align hot counters to cache line (64 bytes)
- Separate read-heavy and write-heavy state

## TESTS (REQUIRED)
1. Unit test: FuseBackend is Send + Sync (compile-time)
2. Integration: 100 concurrent read requests complete without deadlock
3. Integration: 10 concurrent write + 90 concurrent read complete
4. Stress: 10 threads x 1000 ops each, verify no data corruption

## LOGGING
- Log thread pool initialization (thread count)
- Log per-thread request dispatch (trace level)
- Log lock contention events (warn level)

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. `cargo test` confirms FuseBackend: Send + Sync
2. Concurrent benchmark shows >4x throughput vs single-threaded
3. No deadlocks in 1-hour stress test
4. Metrics show balanced load across threads","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:50:19.657268383Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T10:05:04.975125973Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T10:05:04.975035524Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["concurrency","core","fuse"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-20p","depends_on_id":"bd-22w.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:54:39.189131817Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-20p","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:56:37.462340437Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-20p","depends_on_id":"bd-3cjj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:52:34.024839399Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

is the core FrankenFS differentiator.

We start with FCW (simple, testable), then evolve toward SSI only where necessary.
Key integration points:
- MVCC-aware block reads: (snapshot -> version or base)
- Durable version store design
- Deterministic concurrency tests

This track must remain mathematically defensible and heavily tested.","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:34:39Z"}]}
